Test Building

The Test Building was a historic structure located in Melbourne, Victoria, Australia. It was constructed in 1890 and demolished in 1970 to make way for a modern office tower.

The building was notable for its Victorian Italianate architecture and served as a hotel for over seventy years. Local historians have described its demolition as one of Melbourne's great architectural losses.

History

Built during the land boom era, the Test Building exemplified the prosperity of late nineteenth-century Melbourne. The ground floor housed a popular bar, while the upper floors provided accommodation for travellers arriving at nearby Spencer Street Station.
